title: Verification + binary smoke + carry-forward documentation
scope: Final verification of vuln-source-parity-v1 closure: full vuln_migration_v1_red test suite + workspace cargo tests + binary smoke on /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/* fixtures + carry-forward exception documentation across 3 technical buckets.
implemented: Phase A test verification (5 cargo test invocations) + Phase B release binary build + binary smoke loop across 14 fall-through langs + Phase C/D/E report JSON writes (M5-carry-forward.json, M5-binary-smoke.json, M5-report.json). NO source code changes — M5 is verification + docs only.

vuln_migration_v1_red.stop_threshold_revision: Plan projected 165/166 in M5 stop-thresholds. Actual is 158/166 due to M2 surfacing 3 NEW carry-forwards (cpp/java/scala_deserialization_positive) from a distinct technical class (nested-constructor var-extraction) — see M5-carry-forward.json Bucket B. Per validator_mandate carry_forward_max_5 NON-additive-resolution clause: surface explicitly with rationale rather than additively patching to meet threshold. Original 5-cap exceeded but documented; aggregate is 8 across 3 technical buckets (5+3+0).

vuln_autodetect_tests.note: Test target is named vuln_autodetect_tests (suffixed) — initial dispatch contract referenced as vuln_autodetect.

tldr_core_workspace_full.result: 1 unrelated pre-existing failure in tldr-core: ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint (rr_module_function_integ_test.rs:62) — 'expected at least one UserInput source' on ast_only path. NOT a vuln-source-parity-v1 regression. Documented as a pre-existing FAI-extension-v1 carry-forward in continuum/autonomous/field_access_info-extension-v1-plan/reports/M5-report.json: "the only M5-related failure is `ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint` ast_only-path which was ALREADY FAILING at M4 (M1 finding #2: bare `gets` parses as identifier in tree-sitter-ruby — known carry-forward gap untouched by M5).". Source verification: git diff 3127472..c9d75ab on RUBY_AST_SOURCES / Ruby gets-related taint.rs paths shows ZERO functional changes affecting this test.

phase_b_binary_smoke.stop_threshold: tldr binary smoke on /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.* returns 0 findings for all 14 fall-through langs
phase_b_binary_smoke.stop_threshold_caveat: Only 3 of 14 fixtures exist at /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/ (py/go/ts). All 3 returned 0 findings. The 11 missing fixtures are pre-existing artifact gaps (not part of this milestone's deliverables) — their string-literal-FP coverage is exercised by in-tree per-lang fixtures in crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/vuln_migration_v1/<lang>/*string_literal_fp* under vuln_migration_v1_red (all GREEN).

follow_on_milestones: rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 (closes Bucket A Rust subset, design milestone first)
ruby-backtick-extraction-v1 (closes Bucket A Ruby subset, +10-20 LOC)

carry_forward_max_5.exception_rationale: M2 verification surfaced 3 additional carry-forwards from a technically distinct class (nested-constructor var-extraction). Per mandate's NON-additive-resolution clause: surface explicitly with rationale and future-milestone scope rather than over-fitting an additive patch to meet the cap. Documented in M5-carry-forward.json with 3 separate follow-on milestones.
